claim: Use Karpathy's LLM wiki approach: organize knowledge as simple markdown files with an index, let Claude Code read and auto-organize relationships—no vector DB or embeddings needed
actionable: Create /raw and /wiki folders; let Claude Code ingest articles and build relationships; query using markdown files and index links, not embeddings
source_title: Andrej Karpathy Just 10x'd Everyone's Claude Code

claim: LLM wikis scale efficiently for ~100 articles/500K words but become expensive at millions of documents—use semantic search RAG for enterprise-scale knowledge instead
actionable: Use LLM wikis for personal/team knowledge bases; switch to traditional RAG (embeddings, vector DB) for enterprise-scale retrieval
source_title: Andrej Karpathy Just 10x'd Everyone's Claude Code

claim: Claude Code can auto-maintain index files and summaries, build relationships between documents, and perform intelligent Q&A without explicit chunking or embedding strategy
actionable: Let Claude Code organize your markdown wiki without specifying chunking strategy; it will auto-create indexes and relationships
source_title: Andrej Karpathy Just 10x'd Everyone's Claude Code

claim: A 'hot cache' file (500 words of recent context) in your wiki reduces token usage when querying repeatedly—cache the latest information separately from the full wiki
actionable: Create a hot.md file in your wiki that stores the 500 most recent/relevant words; point your agents to check it first before reading full wiki pages
source_title: Andrej Karpathy Just 10x'd Everyone's Claude Code

claim: Run periodic 'linting' on your LLM wiki to find inconsistencies, impute missing data via web searches, and identify new relationships and article gaps
actionable: Schedule Claude Code to lint your wiki weekly; ask it to find data gaps and suggest new articles to improve coverage
source_title: Andrej Karpathy Just 10x'd Everyone's Claude Code

claim: LLM wikis are 95% cheaper on token usage than traditional RAG for small-scale knowledge bases—one user dropped token usage from 383 files to a compact wiki
actionable: Measure token usage before/after switching to LLM wiki; expect 80-95% reduction for team-scale knowledge bases
source_title: Andrej Karpathy Just 10x'd Everyone's Claude Code

claim: Keep LLM wiki structure simple and flat (no deep folders) unless your domain specifically needs hierarchy—unnecessary structure confuses the LLM during organization
actionable: Start with flat markdown structure; only add subfolders if your domain clearly requires them (e.g., separate domains by category)
source_title: Andrej Karpathy Just 10x'd Everyone's Claude Code

claim: You can use the same wiki folder across multiple AI agents by pointing them to the claude.md schema—agents understand the structure and can query/update the wiki independently
actionable: Create one well-documented wiki; reuse it in multiple agent projects by referencing its claude.md and wiki structure
source_title: Andrej Karpathy Just 10x'd Everyone's Claude Code
